source.title: dependency-cruiser Rules Reference
source.summary: Assess whether dependency-cruiser's narrow forbidden-rule surface can truthfully protect the canonical dw-state facade and private implementation split without opening repo-wide linting or dependency-policy work.
source.missionAlignmentHint: Engine structure pressure: protect the canonical dw-state read surface with one truthful boundary proposal only if current repo imports support it.

mission.currentObjective: Build and operate a revenue-generating personal AI workspace that continuously strengthens its own capabilities through mission-driven ingestion, proof-backed adoption, and persistent orchestration.
usefulnessSignals: Useful means anything that materially improves:
capabilityLanes: Directive engine materialization - one reusable executable core that hosts can embed cleanly
Runtime operationalization (Runtime) - converting proven patterns into callable, bounded, measurable runtime behavior
Orchestration reliability (OpenClaw) - persistent, looping, memory-backed coordination across agents and sessions
Discovery as operational front door - mission-aware intake, routing, capability-gap detection, and review-cadence enforcement
Behavior-preserving transformation - same capability, better implementation (speed, cost, reliability, maintainability)
Evaluation quality - dimensional measurement (evaluators), not just binary gates
Unified runtime host (Mission Control) - coherent command surface, less fragmented tooling

openGaps.description: Canonical Directive engine surface is only partially materialized as one reusable executable core with clear lane boundaries and host-adapter seams
openGaps.currentState: A first engine slice exists, but real source intake still falls back to host helpers, Markdown-first assets, and manual post-decision lane handoff
openGaps.desiredState: Engine owns substantially more intake, routing, adaptation/improvement, proof, decision, and handoff state while Discovery, Runtime, and Architecture operate as clear Engine lanes

rationale: Matched open gap gap-directive-engine-materialization (rank 1) as the closest current mission pressure.
Recommended architecture because the source offers one bounded Engine-structure boundary rule rather than reusable runtime capability.
Current repo truth already points at a canonical facade/private split around shared/lib/dw-state.ts, which makes this a structural Architecture question.
Split-case is recommended because the proposed boundary must be validated against the actual repo import graph before any rule proposal is retained.

analysis.missionFitSummary: Assess whether dependency-cruiser's narrow forbidden-rule surface can truthfully protect the canonical dw-state facade and private implementation split without opening repo-wide linting or dependency-policy work.
analysis.primaryAdoptionQuestion: What is the primary adoption target of the extracted value?
analysis.usefulnessRationale: Structural usefulness: the source offers a bounded mechanism for preserving Engine-owned facade/private boundaries around the canonical dw-state read surface.

extractedValue: dependency-cruiser configs center custom dependency rules in the forbidden array and validate configs against a schema.
A narrow facade-boundary rule can be expressed with forbidden plus from.pathNot and to.path in a JavaScript config module.
excludedBaggage: generic dependency policy beyond the dw-state facade

adaptationPlan.directiveOwnedForm: Directive-owned Architecture proposal or boundary-config artifact that protects the canonical dw-state facade only if the current import graph makes that boundary truthful.
adaptedValue: Translate dependency-cruiser rule vocabulary into one Engine-owned boundary proposal around shared/lib/dw-state.ts.
Reject the proposal if current repo truth shows the requested importer set is inaccurate.

improvementGoals: improve engine structure clarity around canonical read surfaces
improve future boundary hardening by grounding rule proposals in actual repo truth
improvementPlan.intendedDelta: Turn an external dependency-rule reference into one bounded Architecture judgment about the dw-state facade boundary.

proofPlan.objective: Prove whether the requested dw-state facade-only importer boundary is truthful enough to express as a bounded Architecture artifact.
requiredEvidence: actual import consumers of shared/lib/dw-state.ts, shared/lib/dw-state/shared.ts, and shared/lib/dw-state/runtime.ts are recorded
the dependency-cruiser rule shape is described in Directive-owned terms
the result clearly states whether the requested boundary matches current repo truth
proofPlan.rollbackPrompt: Keep the result at experiment status and do not integrate any boundary enforcement until the target rule matches current repo truth.

decision.summary: Preliminary engine decision: accept_for_architecture for Architecture, pending bounded truth-check of the requested facade boundary.
rationale: Matched open gap gap-directive-engine-materialization (rank 1) as the closest current mission pressure.
Recommended architecture because the source offers one bounded Engine-structure boundary rule rather than reusable runtime capability.
Current repo truth already points at a canonical facade/private split around shared/lib/dw-state.ts, which makes this a structural Architecture question.
Record one bounded Architecture result deciding whether the requested dw-state facade-only importer boundary is truthful enough to materialize.

integrationProposal.nextAction: Record one bounded Architecture result that either materializes the single dw-state facade-boundary proposal or stops because the requested boundary is false in current repo truth.

reportPlan.summary: Sync the accept_for_architecture decision and bounded dw-state facade-boundary evaluation into Directive Workspace reporting surfaces.
reportPlan.usefulnessRationale: Structural usefulness: the source offers a bounded mechanism for preserving Engine-owned facade/private boundaries around the canonical dw-state read surface.

events.summary: dependency-cruiser rules reference analyzed as one possible boundary-rule source for the dw-state facade.
Candidate routed to architecture with usefulness level structural.
